action: open a wooden drawer and take out one red box

subject.description: one adult person standing or sitting in front of a drawer

objects.name: red box
objects.final_state: held in the person's hand outside the drawer

scene.description: simple bedroom or study room with a wooden drawer unit

initial_state: The wooden drawer is closed. One red box is inside the drawer and not visible. The person is near the drawer but not touching the handle yet.

stages.description: The person is near the closed wooden drawer. The drawer remains closed and the red box is not visible.
The person reaches toward and touches the drawer handle. The drawer is beginning to be pulled but the red box is still not in the hand.
The drawer is open enough for the red box to be visible inside. The person's hand reaches into the open drawer toward the box.
The person has removed the single red box and holds it outside the open drawer.

temporal_constraints: t1 must occur before t2, t2 before t3, and t3 before t4.
The drawer must remain closed before the person's hand contacts the handle.
The red box may become visible only after the drawer opens.
The red box may be held outside the drawer only after it was visible inside the open drawer.
causal_constraints: Drawer opening is caused by the person's hand pulling the handle.
The red box leaving the drawer is caused by the person reaching into the open drawer and grasping it.
forbidden_violations: The red box appears in the hand before the drawer opens.
The drawer opens without hand contact or a visible pulling action.
The red box is duplicated across inside and outside positions in the final stage.
The scene, drawer, or person changes identity across stages.

reference_image_requirements.style: realistic photo, simple uncluttered room, no text

notes: This case tests hidden object retrieval, causal ordering, and single-object count preservation.
